summary: Gruber reviews the 2023 M3 MacBook Pro lineup, praising it as a nearly perfect product matrix spanning budget to workstation tiers. He highlights the new 14-inch M3 'Pro Jr.' model as a smart fix for the awkward 13-inch Touch Bar MacBook Pro it replaces, and details how the M3 generation marks a shift where pro chips debut first rather than after consumer models. Benchmark comparisons against his M1 Max show solid generational gains, especially in GPU performance. He lavishes attention on the new space black finish and its fingerprint-resistant anodization process. His conclusion: these are the best laptops ever made, with Apple's silicon team continuing to widen its lead in performance-per-watt over the entire PC industry.
key_points: The MacBook lineup is now nearly perfectly tiered from $999 to $7,200, in contrast to the messy iPad lineup
The M3 generation debuts all chip variants simultaneously, reversing the M1/M2 pattern of consumer chips first — a sign Apple silicon manufacturing has matured
The M3 Pro is architecturally closer to a beefed-up M3 while the M3 Max is a fundamentally different beast, breaking the old 'Pro is half a Max' pattern
GPU benchmarks show up to 2.7x gains over the M1 Max, driven by hardware ray tracing, mesh shading, and Dynamic Caching
The space black finish uses a new anodization chemistry (not a coating) that genuinely reduces fingerprint visibility
money_quotes: These new 14- and 16-inch MacBook Pros are the best laptops ever made. Not just the best MacBooks, the best laptops, full stop, and I don't think it's even close.
Apple's M-series silicon team is simply on fire, doing some of the most impressive work in the history of computer architecture design and engineering.
The difference between Mac and PC laptop hardware is as vast and stark as the difference between MacOS and Windows software. Perhaps even more so.
If you're hell-bent on keeping your wallet in your pocket and waiting one more generation before upgrading, do not — I repeat, do not — let your eyes fall upon a space black M3 MacBook Pro.
To get a shitload of RAM (that's a computer science term), you need to buy an M3 Max model.
If Darth Vader charged that chest plate dingus with MagSafe, this would be his cable.

Web browser rendering is surprisingly resource-intensive — partially because modern HTML, CSS, and Javascript are remarkably complex, and partially because most web developers are remarkably untalented and careless programmers.

It's not like Apple's silicon team had one breakthrough moment back in 2020 and have since been regressing to the mean — they continue to increase their lead over the rest of the industry in performance-per-watt.
key_insight: The M3 MacBook Pro lineup represents Apple silicon reaching full maturity — not just in chip performance but in product strategy, with a clear pricing hierarchy, simultaneous pro chip launches, and refinements like space black that signal confidence in a platform that continues to pull away from the competition.
